reforms.description: Statistics Norway’s evaluation of the tax-subsidy scheme (Skattefunn) shows it performs well on support for innovation and R&D additionality compared to similar schemes in other countries.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The evaluation indicates that the scheme effectively supports innovation and R&D, which contributes positively to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The evaluation has been completed and reported, indicating that the scheme is currently in operation.
reforms.importance_rationale: The evaluation provides significant insights into the effectiveness of an existing innovation support scheme, indicating its importance in the innovation landscape.
reforms.source_quote: Statistics Norway’s evaluation of tax-subsidy scheme (Skattefunn) shows it performs well on support for innovation and R&D additionality.

reforms.description: The 'Knowledge Promotion' reform was launched in 2006 to enhance fundamental skills in Norwegian education, focusing on individual learning and new curricula with defined competence goals.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing fundamental skills and competencies, the reform aims to improve the quality of education, which is essential for fostering innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been fully integrated into the educational system and is actively being applied.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant overhaul of the educational system aimed at improving skills relevant for innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The ongoing reform 'Knowledge Promotion' was launched in 2006, with the objective of developing 'fundamental skills that will enable pupils to participate actively in Norwegian knowledge society'.
